graphdb_rdf.metric_question: Trace the complete transparency obligation chain for AI-generated content: What must the AI system provider do, what must the deployer do when creating deep fakes, and how does this interact with obligations under the Digital Services Act for very large online platforms?
graphdb_rdf.metric_answer: The transparency obligation chain for AI-generated content, particularly in the context of deep fakes, involves several key responsibilities for both the AI system provider and the deployer, as well as interactions with obligations under the Digital Services Act for very large online platforms. ### 1. Obligations of the AI System Provider The AI system provider is required to ensure transparency regarding the existence of AI-generated or manipulated content. This is encapsulated in the **transparency obligation for deep fakes**. Specifically, the provider must: - Disclose the existence of generated or manipulated content to users, ensuring that users are aware when they are interacting with such content. This is crucial to prevent misinformation and maintain trust. ### 2. Obligations of the Deployer When deploying AI systems that create deep fakes, the deployer must: - Adhere to the transparency obligations set forth by the AI system provider. - Ensure that any content generated is clearly labeled as such, thereby informing users that the content may not be authentic or truthful. ### 3. Interaction with the Digital Services Act (DSA) For **very large online platforms**, there are additional obligations under the Digital Services Act: - These platforms must implement a **risk management framework** that includes identifying and mitigating systemic risks associated with the dissemination of AI-generated content, including deep fakes. - The providers of very large online platforms are obligated to identify and mitigate these systemic risks, which may include the risk of misinformation stemming from deep fakes. In summary, the AI system provider must ensure transparency about AI-generated content, the deployer must label such content appropriately, and both entities must comply with the broader obligations under the Digital Services Act to manage risks associated with the distribution of deep fakes on large online platforms. For more detailed information, you can refer to the specific obligations outlined in the [EU AI Act](https://euaiact.eu/resource/Obligation/transparency_obligation_for_deep_fakes) and the [Digital Services Act](https://euaiact.eu/resource/Product/very_large_online_platforms).
